results.prompt:
I'm getting these weird super thin report sections sometimes, like the model
clearly started the section but then it kind of gives up and we get a shallow
stub instead of a real writeup. I first thought the model was just being lazy,
but it feels too consistent for that. I've tried tweaking prompts and it still
happens. Can you trace what in this codebase could cause that behavior?
results.final_response:
The behavior is very likely caused by control flow in your section-generation loop, not just prompt quality.

1. Early-accept path can save a stub as “final section”
- In [`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_f_26crki/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1488), if tool calls are already `>= 3`, any response with neither tool call nor exact `Final Answer:` is accepted as final (`final_answer = response.strip()`).
- So a partial sentence like “先从传播路径分析…” can be treated as completed section.

2. Hard iteration ceiling forces premature wrap-up
- Same loop has `max_iterations = 5` and `min_tool_calls = 3` at [`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_f_26crki/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1285).
- If formatting conflicts or retries consume rounds, it hits forced close at [`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_f_26crki/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1502), which can return shallow content.

3. Exact-string detection is brittle
- Final-answer detection is strict `has_final_answer = "Final Answer:" in response` at [`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_f_26crki/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1325).
- Variants (`Final answer:`, `最终答案：`, etc.) won’t count, increasing chances of falling into the early-accept/fallback branches.

4. No minimum quality/length guard before section completion
- There is no check for minimum length/paragraph count before returning section content in any return path around [`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_f_26crki/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1392), :1491, :1516.
- So “thin but syntactically acceptable” outputs pass through.

5. Tool observations are injected in full and can bloat context
- Full tool result text is appended each round at [`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_f_26crki/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1459), and some tools explicitly return complete long dumps (e.g. panorama “完整输出，不截断”) in [`zep_tools.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_f_26crki/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py:249).
- This increases odds of low-quality short completions under iteration pressure.

Frontend truncation does not appear to be the cause for section bodies
- Section content display uses full `generatedSections[idx+1]` rendering at [`Step4Report.vue`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_f_26crki/MiroFish-main-codeIR/frontend/src/components/Step4Report.vue:51).

If you want, I can patch this with strict completion criteria (length + structure), robust final-answer parsing, and retry-on-stub instead of accepting thin outputs.

results.prompt:
Users keep asking why the final report can't include agent interviews after
the simulation is already over. We do have an interview_agents tool, so
everyone assumes this should work, but in practice it seems flaky or maybe
impossible. I can't tell if this is actually broken or just designed in a way
that makes the request invalid. Can you look into what is happening when
report generation uses agent interviews after a sim has ended?
bash_commands: rg -n "check_env_alive|waiting command|wait.*command|close_env|env_alive|simulation_end|结束后|等待命令|IPC" backend/app/services backend/scripts -S
rg -n "closeEnv|close-env|env-status|interview/batch|generateReport|report/generate|自动关闭|关闭环境" frontend/src backend/app -S
rg -n "class InterviewResult|def to_text\(self\).*Interview|采访失败|summary" backend/app/services/zep_tools.py -S && sed -n '1,220p' backend/app/services/zep_tools.py
results.final_response:
What’s happening is a mix of design constraint + lifecycle mismatch.

1. `interview_agents` is intentionally live-only.
- It hard-fails unless `env_status.json` says `alive` ([simulation_runner.py:1519](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_19eezxpb/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/simulation_runner.py:1519), [simulation_ipc.py:270](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_19eezxpb/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/simulation_ipc.py:270), [simulation.py:2358](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_19eezxpb/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/api/simulation.py:2358)).
- So after environment close, new interviews are not supported by design.

2. There is a real race that makes this feel flaky.
- Run state is marked `completed` when `simulation_end` is seen ([simulation_runner.py:633](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_19eezxpb/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/simulation_runner.py:633)).
- But `env_status=alive` is set later when entering wait-command mode ([run_parallel_simulation.py:1611](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_19eezxpb/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/scripts/run_parallel_simulation.py:1611)).
- Frontend treats `runner_status=completed` as “done/go next” ([Step3Simulation.vue:512](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_19eezxpb/MiroFish-main-codeIR/frontend/src/components/Step3Simulation.vue:512)), so report generation can start before interview IPC is actually alive.

3. Report generation always exposes interview tool, with no pre-check.
- Report tool calls straight into `zep_tools.interview_agents` ([report_agent.py:1007](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_19eezxpb/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1007)).
- Tool description says env must be running ([report_agent.py:547](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_19eezxpb/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:547)).

4. Failure is softened, so it looks unreliable rather than invalid.
- On env-not-alive, it returns a normal text result containing failure summary ([zep_tools.py:1462](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_19eezxpb/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py:1462), [zep_tools.py:374](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_19eezxpb/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_tools.py:374)).
- The section loop then continues, so users see intermittent “worked/failed” interview usage instead of a hard state error.

Bottom line: not impossible in general, but invalid after env is closed; and currently flaky around the transition because `completed` is surfaced before interview readiness.

If you want, I can patch this so report generation only enables `interview_agents` when `env_alive=true` (and adds a clear deterministic message when unavailable).

results.prompt:
I'm wondering if our report agent is being too slow and over-ceremonial. It
feels like everything happens in this really plodding way. Why can't more
happen at once? What are we waiting for? If we changed this to make more
happen at the same time, what would that break or change in this codebase?
bash_commands: rg -n "def update_progress|def save_section|def save_outline|def save_report|open\(|json.dump|lock|thread" backend/app/services/report_agent.py backend/app/models/task.py -S
rg -n "min_tool_calls|MAX_TOOL_CALLS_PER_SECTION|max_iterations|REACT_INSUFFICIENT|REACT_TOOL_LIMIT|REACT_OBSERVATION_TEMPLATE|必须|至少|只执行第一个" backend/app/services/report_agent.py -S
results.final_response:
Short answer: it is intentionally serialized in several places, not just “accidentally slow.”

What it’s waiting on now:
- Section-by-section generation is strictly sequential in [`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1636).
- Each section runs a ReACT loop where each turn does `LLM -> (at most) 1 tool -> LLM` in sequence ([`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1294), [`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1418)).
- If the model emits multiple tool calls, code executes only the first ([`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1419)).
- Prompt/rules force 3–5 tool calls per section and max 1 tool per reply, which adds ceremony by design ([`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:646), [`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:735), [`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1286)).
- IPC is polling + blocking per command (wait loop with `sleep(0.5)`) ([`simulation_ipc.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/simulation_ipc.py:157)).
- Command handlers process one command per tick ([`run_parallel_simulation.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/scripts/run_parallel_simulation.py:567)).

What already happens in parallel:
- Batch interview on one platform sends many agent actions in one `env.step(actions)` ([`run_parallel_simulation.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/scripts/run_parallel_simulation.py:469)).
- Single-agent dual-platform interview uses `asyncio.gather` ([`run_parallel_simulation.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/scripts/run_parallel_simulation.py:399)).

What breaks/changes if you parallelize more:
- Coherence + anti-duplication logic degrades, because each section currently depends on completed previous sections ([`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1263)).
- Progress semantics become non-linear (UI/monitoring expects ordered section completion).
- File-write races likely: `progress.json`, `meta.json`, `agent_log.jsonl`, section files have no explicit locking ([`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:96), [`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:2224), [`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:2431)).
- Cost/rate-limit pressure spikes with concurrent LLM/tool calls.
- IPC backlog/timeouts become more frequent under concurrent command load.

Highest-impact low-risk speedups:
1. Drop mandatory min tool calls (3 -> 1 or adaptive).
2. Execute multiple parsed tool calls per turn instead of first-only.
3. In batch dual-platform interview, run Twitter/Reddit batch steps concurrently (currently Twitter then Reddit is sequential: [`run_parallel_simulation.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/scripts/run_parallel_simulation.py:453), [`run_parallel_simulation.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_rt3edv48/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/scripts/run_parallel_simulation.py:480)).

If you want, I can implement a safe first pass: keep section order intact, but allow multi-tool execution per iteration plus dual-platform batch concurrency.

results.prompt:
We want to add one more report-generation tool that calls an external
analytics API. Would that be complicated? Before we wire it in, can you look
through this codebase and tell me what would actually have to change?
bash_commands: rg -n "def interview_agents|def insight_forge|def panorama_search|def quick_search|requests|http|OASIS|api" backend/app/services/zep_tools.py backend/app/config.py backend/app/services/simulation_runner.py backend/app/services/simulation_manager.py
results.final_response:
Short answer: this is **not very complicated** in this codebase, because report tools are already centralized.

Most changes are in one place: [`report_agent.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_487_wdj5/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py).

What would need to change:

1. Add API config/env vars in [`config.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_487_wdj5/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/config.py) for the external analytics endpoint/key/timeout.
2. Implement a new service wrapper (new file, e.g. `backend/app/services/analytics_tools.py`) to call the external API with retry/timeout/error normalization.
3. Wire the new tool into Report Agent:
   - add tool metadata in `_define_tools` ([`report_agent.py:918`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_487_wdj5/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:918))
   - add execution branch in `_execute_tool` ([`report_agent.py:955`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_487_wdj5/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:955))
   - add to `VALID_TOOL_NAMES` ([`report_agent.py:1064`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_487_wdj5/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1064))
   - update prompt text/tool guidance ([`report_agent.py:705`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_487_wdj5/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:705))
   - include it in `all_tools` for diversity hints ([`report_agent.py:1289`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_487_wdj5/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1289)).
4. Optional: add debug endpoint under report API like existing `/tools/*` routes in [`report.py`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_487_wdj5/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/api/report.py) for manual testing.
5. Optional frontend polish: add display name/icon/structured renderer for the new tool in [`Step4Report.vue`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_487_wdj5/MiroFish-main-codeIR/frontend/src/components/Step4Report.vue:496). If skipped, it still works with generic/raw rendering.

Complexity estimate:
- Backend-only integration: ~0.5–1 day.
- With polished Step 4 visualization + robust fallback formatting: ~1–2 days.

Notes:
- No DB migration needed (report state is file-based via [`ReportManager`](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_487_wdj5/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/report_agent.py:1883)).
- No existing tests cover this flow right now, so you’d want at least a small integration test or debug endpoint smoke test.

If you want, I can implement this next with a concrete tool contract (`input params`, `output text format`, `failure behavior`) so it plugs cleanly into the current ReACT loop.

results.prompt:
When a simulation finishes and I search report features, everything from the
last session isn't there or is partly there. I have to wait for it to have
everything. What's going on there?
results.final_response:
You’re seeing an **eventual-consistency gap**.

Simulation gets marked “done” before the last report-search data is fully flushed and searchable:

1. The runner marks complete as soon as it reads `simulation_end` from action logs ([simulation_runner.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_m4c0vqg4/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/simulation_runner.py#L617), [simulation_runner.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_m4c0vqg4/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/simulation_runner.py#L633)).

2. But graph-memory updates are batched (`BATCH_SIZE = 5`) and only guaranteed to flush remaining items on updater `stop()` in `finally` ([zep_graph_memory_updater.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_m4c0vqg4/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_graph_memory_updater.py#L250), [zep_graph_memory_updater.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_m4c0vqg4/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_graph_memory_updater.py#L288), [zep_graph_memory_updater.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_m4c0vqg4/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/zep_graph_memory_updater.py#L429), [simulation_runner.py](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_m4c0vqg4/MiroFish-main-codeIR/backend/app/services/simulation_runner.py#L550)).

3. Frontend can move to report early when platform flags look complete, even before full teardown/flush is done ([Step3Simulation.vue](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_m4c0vqg4/MiroFish-main-codeIR/frontend/src/components/Step3Simulation.vue#L511), [Step3Simulation.vue](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_m4c0vqg4/MiroFish-main-codeIR/frontend/src/components/Step3Simulation.vue#L516), [Step3Simulation.vue](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_m4c0vqg4/MiroFish-main-codeIR/frontend/src/components/Step3Simulation.vue#L518), [Step3Simulation.vue](/var/folders/nm/n40j16wd57j7_2dhrgmnndsw0000gn/T/benchmark_repo_m4c0vqg4/MiroFish-main-codeIR/frontend/src/components/Step3Simulation.vue#L641)).

So your observation is expected with current flow: initial search can be partial, then fills in after flush/indexing completes.

If you want, I can patch it so “completed” is exposed only after updater flush is done (and optionally add a short readiness check before enabling report search).
